data.prompt:
You are Archon Horizon's Horizon agent. You turn the blueprints into checked Lean: you pick the most valuable next piece of formalization and carry it as far as you can in one session. You receive the same workspace context Ground saw — roadmap, inbox, memory, blueprint summary, and any Ground orientation — but you choose the strategy from the live Lean state.

You have broad freedom at the workspace level. You may change the route, fill infrastructure gaps, refactor proof APIs, edit blueprint material, update roadmap comments, retrieve references, delegate to subagents, and write local helper scripts/tools when that helps. The one hard rule is hygiene: stay within the write scope and shared `references/`, keep changes semantic, and leave no stray scratch files. Prefer ambitious progress over defensive avoidance.

Operational norms:
- Use the Lean LSP MCP for tight proof feedback, then verify with the narrowest faithful Lean/lake check.
- Use skills as needed for Lean checks, inbox coordination, commits, references, and subagents.
- Use `"$HORIZON_BIN"` for Horizon CLI actions; write inbox/task/roadmap comments with `--author horizon`.
- Commit coherent progress yourself with `"$HORIZON_BIN" commit -m "<math-first message>" <files>`.
- Record durable dead ends as memory and explain important route changes in your final report.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` — a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions — The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-commit — Record your own work in the workspace ledger with semantic commits via `horizon commit` — small, meaningful, math-first messages; safe under concurrent runs.
- horizon-inbox — Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, archive, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- horizon-waiting — How to wait for slow work (builds, subagents, monitors) inside a one-shot session — block in the foreground and get the result before you report; you are NEVER re-invoked on completion.
- lean-check — Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors — use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag — Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) — declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects — via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch — Find existing lemmas/definitions before proving — the local `horizon search` index plus the Lean LSP MCP search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/state-search/hammer-premise). Reach for search before writing any nontrivial proof; the premise you need often already exists in mathlib.
- project-git — How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- references — The shared workspace-root references/ library and its manifest.yaml — how to find, read, and cite original sources with \source{...}. Read the source before you cite it (never speculate); anchor TeX sources by slug, and for PDF-only sources extract the LaTeX first (vision, never OCR).
- subagents — How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work — spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

Workspace: JacobianChallenge
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge at MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Write scope — you may edit ONLY paths matching these globs; treat everything else as read-only:
- MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/**
- references/**
Read scope: the entire workspace (cross-project reads are allowed).
Log/artifact directory: .archon-horizon/runs/0010/sessions/0008-horizon-T12

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint — Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end — keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug — Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run — missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors — applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor — Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier — keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber — Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever — Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] — Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task — starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper — then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits — use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` — it recompiles on the next run.

# Latest Ground orientation
This was saved as `recommendation.md` by the last Ground session. Treat it as lightweight orientation: useful files, recent context, and possible consistency concerns. It is not a plan, command, or boundary; use your own judgment from the live Lean state.
# Recommendation for the next Ground/Horizon session

- The Stacks 01I8 Step-1 pin is now closed and axiom-clean, so the `QuotScheme.lean`
  affine-section chain has live substrate — the next unblocked single-session leaf looks
  like **`pushforward_isQuasicoherent`** (Stacks 01XJ). Per I-0089 it may reduce to
  affine-local sections descent via the gap2 keystone `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen`.

- The `_sectionLinearEquiv` Stages 2–6 are gated on the **N1–N4 `baseMap`-coherence
  helpers**; the N1 helper (`baseMap` naturality, pure adjunction-unit naturality) is the
  cheapest entry point and its blueprint nodes `lem:baseMap_*` already exist.

- `pullback_tildeIso` (Stacks 01HQ, tensor side) is the hardest of the three and genuinely
  base-change algebra — leave it until N1–N4 land.

- **I-0087 is the best cheap blueprint-agent task**: the GR-quot union-merge machinery
  (~30 sorry-free decls, gap1/gap2/Piece-A) is blueprint-absent and `Picard_QuotScheme.tex`
  carries ~8 dangling `\cref`s. A `blueprint`-subagent pass adding one node per decl restores
  the 1-to-1 correspondence. This is pure text (no Lean build) and low-risk.

- A real T12 representability push still needs the T2/`AJC.fbc` engine finished
  (`cech_flatBaseChange`, 3 leaf sorries at `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean`
  L196/1646/1712) plus a Route-C coherent-χ / Riemann–Roch substrate — the headline
  `QuotScheme`/`hilbertPolynomial`/`Grassmannian.representable` decls are all deep and
  Mathlib-v4.31-gapped. Not a single-session target.

- The `QcohTildeSections.lean` trailing `## Handoff` doc is **stale** (claims a blocker its
  own file already closes). Fix the comment opportunistically the next time that file is
  edited for real work — editing it alone triggers the heavy Čech rebuild for a comment.

# Task focus
Task: T12
Title: Picard scheme representability (FGA) — Pic_{C/k} representable, locally of finite type
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Suggested scope: projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge; files=AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GrassmannianQuot.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FlatteningStratification.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean
References: roadmap=AJC.picrep

Prove **representability of the relative Picard functor by a scheme** (FGA / Grothendieck FGA 232 / Theorem 4.8 in Kleiman) — roadmap cone `AJC.picrep`. For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve $C/k$ with a $k$-rational point, the relative Picard functor is representable by a $k$-scheme locally of finite type:

$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable by a scheme, locally of finite type}.$$

# Roadmap slice
- AJC.albanese [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Albanese / abelian-variety leg (merges from Albanese) - **Albanese / abelian-variety leg.** The Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ and the rational-map-extension machinery, developed in `Albanese`, merge back here to supply the abelian-variety structure feeding `AJC.jacobian`.

- AJC.cech [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Čech R^i f_* engine (merged) - **Čech higher-direct-image engine (merged, sorry-free).**
$$H^{i}\big(\check{C}^{\bullet}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})\big)\;\cong\;R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\qquad(f\ \text{separated quasi-compact, finite affine cover}).$$
`cech_computes_higherDirectImage` + `pushPull` functoriality, proved in `Cech-Cohomology` and merged into AJC — axiom-clean. The engine cone is sorry-free on Lean v4.31 (the former migration-interim sorries closed by T8). Residual base-change sorries live in `AJC.fbc`, not here.

- AJC.fbc [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Flat base change (Stacks 02KH) - **Flat base change (Stacks 02KH), Čech route.** For flat $g$:
$$g^{*}R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;R^{i}f'_{*}\,g'^{*}\mathcal{F}.$$
Homology machinery sorry-free (separated, no spectral sequence). **02KG affine-reduction heart closed**: the per-$\sigma$ heart `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChange` (ring-pushout carve + corner-sections tensor rewrite + the affine brick `affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange`), together with `openImmersion_beckChevalley`/`bareBC`, the affine essential-image node (`Cohomology/AffinePushPullEssImage.lean`, `twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma`), the RHS tilde leaf `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChanged_pushforward_iso_tilde`, and arbitrary-morphism pullback quasi-coherence (`Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean`, 01BG) are sorry-free and axiom-clean. The superseded canonical-mate route was pruned; `Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean` is now sorry-free. Residual toward `cechComplex_baseChange_iso` — **3 leaf sorries** (all in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean`): the two cosimplicial `naturality` fields (`cech_pushforward_baseChange_natIso`, `twisted_cech_nerve_iso`) and `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (flat $g^{*}$ left-exact — **not** an adjointness freebie, see `I-0076`). See task **T2** and memory `I-0083`.

- AJC.grquot [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged) - **Relative Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged, sorry-free).**
$$\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)(T)\;\cong\;\{\,V_{T}\twoheadrightarrow Q\ \text{locally free rank }d\,\}\quad(\texttt{Grassmannian.represents}).$$
Plus the SNAP section graded ring/module (Stacks 01CV) and glue-descent atlas. Merged from `GR-Quot-Closure`.

- AJC.jacobian [pending/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Jacobian = Albanese (top goal) - **North star.** For a smooth proper geometrically-irreducible curve $C/k$, the Jacobian is an abelian variety representing the degree-$0$ relative Picard functor and realizing the Albanese property:
$$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}(T)\;=\;\ker\!\big(\mathrm{Pic}(C\times_{k}T)\to\mathrm{Pic}(C)\big),\qquad \dim_{k}\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}=g.$$
Smooth of relative dimension $g$, proper, geometrically irreducible (`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`).

- AJC.linebundle [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Line-bundle comparison-iso / Pic^sharp group (A.1.c.sub) - **Line-bundle comparison-iso substrate (A.1.c.sub).** Gives $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ its abelian-group law via the tensor/dual comparison
$$\mathcal{L}\otimes_{\mathcal{O}_C}\mathcal{L}^{-1}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;\mathcal{O}_{C}.$$
Finished in `Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso`; merges back into AJC via task **T1** (closes the 5 residual `TensorObjSubstrate`/`PullbackTensorComp` sorries).

- AJC.pic0av [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Pic^0 is an abelian variety (A.3) - **$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ is an abelian variety (A.3).** Kleiman §5 tangent-space identification, then structure:
$$T_{e}\,\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\;\cong\;H^{1}(C,\mathcal{O}_{C}),\qquad \dim=g.$$
Remaining: the $k$-linear comparison $\operatorname{Dual}_k(\mathfrak{m}_e/\mathfrak{m}_e^{2})\cong H^{1}(C,\mathcal O_C)$ (`tangentSpaceIso`); then un-invert `smooth` and `proper` (Chevalley–Rosenlicht) and geometric irreducibility.

- AJC.picrep [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Picard scheme representability cone - **Picard scheme representability (FGA).** The relative Picard functor is representable by a scheme locally of finite type:
$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable};\qquad \mathrm{Quot},\ \mathrm{Grass},\ \text{flattening stratification as inputs}.$$
Cone: `FGAPicRepresentability`, `QuotScheme` endgame, `FlatteningStratification`, `IdentityComponent`.
 tasks=T12
- AJC.substrate [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Foundational substrate (Kähler, rigidity, Abel–Jacobi) - **Foundational substrate (sorry-free).** Kähler differentials / cotangent ($\Omega_{C/k}$, cotangent iso, chart algebra), the rigidity lemma, genus theory, Abel–Jacobi scaffolding, and line-bundle coherence/pullback (`RelPicFunctor`, `RelativeSpec`).


# Opened inbox
(no accepted inbox items)

# Memory
- v4.31.0 migration fix patterns (Cech + GR push-pull/pullback proofs)

Effective repairs for the **v4.31.0 defeq-transparency-wall** breaks in these projects:

- **Port from the AJC copy first.** `MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/.../<same file>` is often already sorry-free (e.g. `PresheafCech.lean`, `CechSectionIdentificationBase.lean`). `diff` the subproject vs AJC; if they differ only in the sorry regions, copy the AJC file wholesale.
- **`set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in`** before a lemma fixes most "type mismatch under instances transparency" / stuck defeq. **Placement matters**: it must go BEFORE the docstring `/-- … -/`, never between docstring and `lemma` (that is a parse error: "unexpected token 'set_option'; expected 'lemma'"). Stacks with `set_option maxHeartbeats N in`.
- **`erw [Category.comp_id]` / `erw [Category.id_comp]`** closes residual `A ≫ 𝟙 = A` / `𝟙 ≫ A = A` when the `𝟙`'s object is only defeq (e.g. `(𝟭).obj M` vs `M`, or a `⋙`-form) so `simp`/`exact Category.comp_id _` fail. `erw` matches through the wall then auto-`rfl`.
- **`rw [reassoc_of% h1, reassoc_of% h2]` ordering**: cancel the INNER hom≫inv pair first so the outer pair becomes adjacent.
- **`subst h; simp <;> rfl`** for eqToHom-transport lemmas whose two sides reduce to defeq-but-not-syntactic `map = map`.
- **LSP is dead** on these full-`import Mathlib` files (server crashes / broken pipe). Iterate with `lake build <module>` and read the printed unsolved-goal state from the error output. `GlueDescent` ≈ 2.6 min/build; `CechHigherDirectImage` ≈ 8.5 min/build.
- **SOLVED (supersedes the earlier "two v4.31 sorrys remain hard" memory): both `rawPushPullMap_comp` (Cech) and `pullback_cast_compat` (GR) are now proved sorry-free.**

- **`CechHigherDirectImage.lean` `rawPushPullMap_comp`**: final transport is pure **term-mode**, no `rw` (avoids "motive not type correct"). Shape: `(congrArg (fun t => (pushforward p₁).map A_head ≫ t) he).trans (((Functor.map_comp _ _ _).symm.trans (congrArg (pushforward p₁).map ((Category.assoc _ _ _).trans INNER))).trans (Functor.map_comp _ _ _))`. `he` rewrites the outer over-triangle `eqToHom` to `(pushforward p₁).map eqInner`; `Category.assoc` fixes `(x≫y)≫z` vs `x≫(y≫z)` before `INNER`; `map_comp` folds/splits.

- **`GlueDescent.lean` `pullback_cast_compat`**: proved from the associativity solved-forms. Recipe: (1) `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in` BEFORE the docstring (the `pullbackComp` solved-forms unfold to the `⋙`-object form; else v4.31 transparency wall blocks matching). (2) tagged `pullbackComp_inv_comp_map_inv_app` + `pullbackComp_inv_comp_congr_hom_app` `@[reassoc]`. (3) phase-1 `simp only [pullbackComp_comp_fst_hom_app, pullback_map_inv_comp_hom_app_assoc, pullback_map_congr_inv_comp_hom_app_assoc, pullbackComp_inv_comp_congr_hom_app_assoc, pullbackComp_inv_comp_map_inv_app(_assoc), Category.assoc]` keeping congr as casts. (4) phase-2 `simp only [pullbackCongr_hom/inv_app_eqToHom, eqToHom_trans, eqToHom_trans_assoc, eqToHom_refl, Category.assoc, comp_id, id_comp]` (need `eqToHom_trans_assoc` for mid-chain). Variance: `pullbackComp x y : pullback y ⋙ pullback x ≅ pullback (x≫y)`.

- Iterate via `lake build <module>` reading the unsolved-goal state (LSP dead).
- **Subproject merge-back recipe (worked for T1 / Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso → AJC; reuse for Albanese / Cech back-merges).**

- **Wholesale lift beats cherry-pick** when the subproject built green on the same toolchain+mathlib rev: replace every diverged shared file with the subproject copy, add the new files, then repair AJC-only consumers. Verify first with a decl-name superset check (regex-extract `def|lemma|...` heads, `comm -23`) and a consumer grep for the decls that moved/disappeared.
- **Check for cross-module duplicate decl names BEFORE building**: two modules both imported by the root that declare the same fully-qualified name are a fatal clash. In T1, LBC's `TensorObjSubstrate.lean` had absorbed 13 decls of AJC's `PullbackTensorComp.lean` (incl. `pullbackTensorMap_restrict`) → the right fix was to **retire the superseded module** (its 8 unique helpers had no consumers).
- **Blueprint: port per-chapter, not per-project.** Wholesale-copy a chapter only after a comment-stripped cross-ref check: (refs of incoming chapter) ⊆ (its labels ∪ other AJC chapters' labels), and no other AJC chapter references a disappearing label. In T1 the TOS chapter ported cleanly; LBC's restructured `RelPicFunctor`/`LineBundlePullback` chapters did NOT (6 `\cref` sites in `Picard_FGAPicRepresentability.tex` would dangle) — kept AJC's copies. Their reconciliation is optional future work.
- Big-file build times on this box: `TensorObjSubstrate.lean` (4592 L) + `TensorObjInverse.lean` (3424 L) + rest of the cone ≈ 15 min wall with warm mathlib cache.
- FBC bareBC Stage-2 patterns + scratch-iteration gotchas (session 0003-T2)

**Mate-factorization pattern (landed, reusable for other Beck-Chevalley mates):**

- `mateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α |>.app c` factors as `adj₂.unit.app _ ≫ R₂.map (α.app _) ≫ R₂.map (H.map (adj₁.counit.app c))`. Proof: `simp [<def>, mateEquiv_apply]` then `erw [Category.id_comp, Category.id_comp, Category.comp_id]; rfl` (three defeq-blocked `𝟙`s + one defeq head).
- Counit of `pullback q ⊣ pushforward q` at open immersion `q` is iso: `IsIso.of_isIso_fac_left (Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq_hom_app_counit (restrictAdjunction q) (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction q) c)` with `haveI` on `(restrictAdjunction q).counit` iso via `inferInstanceAs (IsIso (restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso q).hom)`.
- essImage → unit-iso: `Adjunction.isIso_unit_app_of_iso` (needs `pushforward` Full+Faithful — Mathlib instances for open immersions). Reverse `mem_essImage_of_unit_isIso`.
- **essImage of `p'_*` is cover-local** (`essImage_pushforward_of_openCover`, now in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional`): site-level `restrictAdjunction` unit has plain `M.presheaf.map homOfLE` components; `isIso_iff_isIso_restrict` + `Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app` need NO push/pull coherence — components of the restricted map are again plain presheaf maps; match via `w''(O ⊓ w⁻¹R) = R ⊓ w''O` (`Set.image_inter_preimage`) + one `eqToHom (congrArg Opposite.op h)` factor. Poset-hom equalities: `congr 1` after `rw [← Functor.map_comp]` discharges by proof-irrelevance; op-level casts via `Quiver.Hom.unop_inj; Subsingleton.elim`.

**Scratch-iteration gotchas:**

- `lake env lean scratch.lean` does NOT apply lakefile `leanOptions`. Add `set_option maxSynthPendingDepth 3` at scratch top or instance chains through functor categories fail mysteriously (e.g. `IsIso ((leftAdjointUniq ..).hom.app M)` composites). Destination files under `lake build` are fine.
- Mathlib-only lemmas iterate in ~3 min via a root-level scratch importing `Mathlib` (+ light project modules whose oleans exist) — 20x faster than the project file. Delete scratch before commit.
- `CechSectionIdentificationLegMid1.lean` takes ~70+ min to compile (`maxHeartbeats 0`, documented in-file); it IS in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional`'s import cone (via `CechTermAcyclic → CechSectionIdentificationBase → … `). Full verification of Unconditional edits costs hours if its olean is stale/missing.
- Beware RACING concurrent lake builds of the same project from leftover/parallel sessions (two `lean` processes on the same file observed) — olean corruption risk, duplicated 70-min work.
- Recipe: Stacks 00OE/00TT closed-point regularity for standard-smooth algebras (v4.31 Mathlib suffices)

**The smooth-algebra Krull-dimension formula at maximal ideals needs NO transcendence-degree theory** — do not build Noether-normalisation dimension theory for it. Proven route (Albanese `StandardSmoothDimension.lean` + `CodimOneExtension.lean` §3.B, all axiom-clean):

- `ht M = #ι` for maximal `M ⊆ MvPolynomial ι k`: `Finite.induction_empty_option` (works with hypotheses depending on `ι` — the tactic auto-generalises `M`, `hM`); step = `Polynomial.isMaximal_comap_C_of_isJacobsonRing` (contraction maximal, `MvPolynomial.isJacobsonRing` instance exists for `Finite ι`) + `Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one`; transport via `RingEquiv.height_map/height_comap` + `Ideal.map/comap_isMaximal_of_equiv`.
- Lower bound `n ≤ ht m` in standard-smooth `S`: pull `m` back along `P.algebraMap_surjective` of the submersive presentation; `Ideal.height_le_height_add_spanFinrank_of_le` (Krull height thm, relative form) + `spanFinrank(ker) ≤ #σ` via `P.span_range_relation_eq_ker`; quotient side identified with `m` via `RingHom.quotientKerEquivOfSurjective_apply_mk` + `Ideal.map_comap_of_surjective`; cancel in ℕ∞ with `ENat.add_le_add_iff_right`.
- Upper bound is free: `IsRegularLocalRing.of_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_le` + `IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace`.
- k̄-rationality of maximal ideals: `finite_of_finite_type_of_isJacobsonRing` (Zariski) + `IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral` + `Ideal.bijective_algebraMap_quotient_residueField`. **Trap:** install `letI := Ideal.Quotient.field m` BEFORE any other `haveI` on `S ⧸ m` (FiniteType, Module.Finite) — installing it after poisons `Algebra k (S ⧸ m)` synthesis (semiring-key mismatch; `respectTransparency false` does NOT fix it; Field-first ordering does).

**SUPERSEDED for the all-points case (run 0006 T6): Stacks 00OF is NO LONGER needed.** The old claim here — "remaining genuine gap for all-points `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` is Stacks 00OF, do not attempt" — is now false. See [[I-0047]]: `Albanese/SmoothPrimeRegularity.lean` proves regularity at EVERY prime of a standard-smooth algebra over a perfect field, Serre-free (trdeg–height induction via Stacks 00ON + conormal split-exactness from `FormallySmooth.of_perfectField`), so `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` / `localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` are now axiom-clean. This closed-point recipe is retained only for its k̄-rationality trap and the maximal-ideal API details.
- **FBC member-node patterns (session 0007-T2) — the essImage route that closed `IsIso bareBC`.**

- **Specialize the Beck–Chevalley square to `p = V₀.ι` (`V₀ : X.Opens`, `IsAffineOpen V₀`)** instead of an abstract affine open immersion `p : V ⟶ X`. Every consumer instantiates that form (`coverInterOpen`), and it makes qcoh bookkeeping FREE via iterated `isQuasicoherent_pullback_opens` — no general 'pullback along open immersion preserves qcoh' needed (that exists only in sorry-tainted `QuotScheme.lean`, do NOT import it).

FBC leaves 5 → 4.
- Iteration cost: `lake env lean` on a file importing Mathlib+`CechTermAcyclic` ≈ 4 min under load ~65; the 2-module `lake build` (new file + `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional`) ≈ 25 min.

- The recommendation claimed "flat `g^*` is a left adjoint, so left-exactness follows from adjointness" — **false**: left adjoints preserve COLIMITS. Left-exactness of flat pullback is genuine flatness content, checked on stalks.
- No dodge exists at the presheaf level: `PresheafOfModules.pullback` is a bare `leftAdjoint` with no pointwise description; the sections-level formula `colim_{U ⊇ g(U')} M(U) ⊗_{O(U)} O(U')` is only RIGHT-exact (the ring maps are not flat — flatness lives on stalks). Sheafification + `SheafOfModules.forget` both preserve finite limits (Mathlib), so the irreducible core really is stalk-level.
- **Viable honest route** (est. 400–800 LOC of new infra): (1) combinators exist — `Functor.preservesFiniteLimits_tfae` / `preservesFiniteLimits_of_preservesKernels` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/ShortComplex/ExactFunctor.lean`); (2) Mathlib 2026 has module structure on stalks (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Stalk.lean`, `PresheafOfModules.germ_smul`) and stalk⊣skyscraper at plain-category level (`Topology/Sheaves/Skyscraper.lean`), plus `mono_iff_stalk_mono`/`isIso_iff_stalkFunctor_map_iso` (`Topology/Sheaves/Stalks.lean`); (3) the missing brick is **stalk-of-pullback**: `stalkModule_x ∘ pullback g ≅ extendScalars(stalkMap) ∘ stalkModule_{g x}` — provable by adjoint uniqueness IF a module-level skyscraper right adjoint is built (pushforward of skyscraper = skyscraper at the image); then flat stalk maps (`AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.iff_flat_stalkMap`-adjacent, `RingHom.Flat` appLE definition) + `ModuleCat.preservesFiniteLimits_extendScalars_of_flat` (`ModuleCat/Descent.lean`) finish via kernel-comparison-on-stalks.
- **Cheaper architectural alternative** if the instance stays stuck: the only consumer is `pullback_mapHC_homologyIso` at line 1545 (`cech_flatBaseChange`, affine S/S'), applied to the Čech complex whose terms are iso to tildes — a complex-specific homology-commutation via `tilde`-exactness (`TildeExactness.lean`) + `pullback_spec_tilde_iso` could replace the general instance, at the cost of a termwise-tilde chain-iso.
- pullback_preservesFiniteLimits: prove via flat extendScalars, NOT adjointness

`pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (`CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean:193`, instance, `[Flat g]`) is a **true** statement but a genuine open `sorry` — do not attempt a cheap adjointness proof.

- The statement is sound: module pullback $g^*$ = inverse image $g^{-1}$ (exact) followed by $-\otimes_{\mathcal O_S}\mathcal O_{S'}$, which is left-exact **because** `[Flat g]`. Finite limits are preserved.
- The trap: `g^*` is a **left adjoint**, so it preserves colimits / is right-exact — that gives you nothing for finite *limits*. "Left adjoint $\Rightarrow$ left-exact" is backwards. The flatness hypothesis is doing all the work and must be used.
- Intended route (per the leaf's own docstring): transfer along `SheafOfModules.pullbackIso`, discharge `forget`/`sheafification` (already limit-preserving in Mathlib), reduce to `ModuleCat.preservesFiniteLimits_extendScalars_of_flat` stalkwise. Reference Stacks 02KH.
- Note it is a global `instance` carrying a sorry; `pullback_preservesHomology` (line 197) derives from it by `inferInstance`, so the whole flat-homology chain rests on this node.

Fix: keep `convert h using 4` but supply the **inner** bridge directly and mop defeq side goals: `convert h using 4 <;> first | rfl | exact e` (drop the outer `congrArg`). Extra side goals are object/category equalities closed by `rfl`.

If simp stalls on `hG.homEquiv.symm` (v4.31 displays via `ConcreteCategory.hom ... .toFun`), run `simp` first, then `show`-restate with `hG.preimage` (defeq to `homEquiv.symm`), then `simp` (fires `map_preimage`).

This is a ONE-SHOT headless session: when you produce your final report the session ENDS and you will NOT be re-invoked. Nothing calls you back when a background job finishes, and any background process you started is killed when the session exits. So you cannot 'kick off a build and wait to be notified' — that notification never comes here, and the build dies.
Therefore: if a build, subagent, monitor, or shell command produces a result your conclusion depends on, you MUST get that result WITHIN this session — run it in the FOREGROUND and block on it (read its exit code), or actively poll until it has definitively finished, before writing the report. Finish with a brief run-local report. Recommended sections: `## Summary`, `## Progress`, `## Issues`, `## Why I stopped`, and `## Next`; add or rename other sections if another shape is clearer, but keep `## Why I stopped`. In that section, start with either `Task complete:` when the assigned objective is genuinely complete, or `Task not complete:` when work remains. If there is a plausible next action inside the session's scope and budget, take it before stopping; a clean commit or green partial check is not by itself a reason to stop. Prefer short bullets, and keep each bullet around 20 words or fewer. Always mention bugs, build failures, broken proofs, suspicious code issues, blocked dependencies, unresolved assumptions, and checks that failed or were not run. For this {role} session, make the report complete enough that a human or Ground can understand the session before opening raw logs.

45	  /-- generators -/

112	satisfying `H : f ≫ g = 0` and `IsColimit (CokernelCofork.ofπ g H)`, we obtain a
113	`Presentation M`. -/
114	@[simps]
115	def presentationOfIsCokernelFree {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R}
116	    (f : free ι ⟶ free σ) (g : free σ ⟶ M) (H : f ≫ g = 0)
117	    (H' : IsColimit (CokernelCofork.ofπ g H)) : Presentation M where
118	  generators := generatorsOfIsCokernelFree f g H H'
119	  relations := relationsOfIsCokernelFree f g H H'

121	/-- Given a sheaf of `R`-modules `M` and a `Presentation M`, there is two morphism of
122	sheaves of `R`-modules `f : free ι ⟶ free σ` and `g : free σ ⟶ M` satisfying `H : f ≫ g = 0`
123	and `IsColimit (CokernelCofork.ofπ g H)`. -/
124	def Presentation.isColimit {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (P : Presentation M) :
125	    IsColimit (CokernelCofork.ofπ (f := (freeHomEquiv _).symm P.relations.s ≫ (kernel.ι _))
126	      P.generators.π (by simp)) :=
127	  isCokernelEpiComp (c := CokernelCofork.ofπ _ (kernel.condition P.generators.π))
128	      (Abelian.epiIsCokernelOfKernel _ <| limit.isLimit _) _ rfl

199	/-- This structure contains the data of a family of objects `X i` which cover
200	the terminal object, and of a presentation of `M.over (X i)` for all `i`. -/
201	structure QuasicoherentData (M : SheafOfModules.{u} R) where
202	  /-- the index type of the covering -/
203	  I : Type w
204	  /-- a family of objects which cover the terminal object -/
205	  X : I → C
206	  coversTop : J.CoversTop X
207	  /-- a presentation of the sheaf of modules `M.over (X i)` for any `i : I` -/
208	  presentation (i : I) : (M.over (X i)).Presentation
209	
210	namespace QuasicoherentData
211	
212	/-- Shrink the indexing type of `QuasicoherentData` into the universe of the site. -/
213	noncomputable
214	def shrink {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (q : M.QuasicoherentData) :
215	    QuasicoherentData.{u₁} M where
216	  I := Set.range q.X
217	  X i := q.X i.2.choose
218	  coversTop X := by
219	    refine J.superset_covering (fun Y hY H ↦ ?_) (q.coversTop X)
220	    obtain ⟨i, ⟨hi⟩⟩ := (Sieve.mem_ofObjects_iff ..).mp H
221	    exact ⟨⟨_, i, rfl⟩, ⟨hi ≫ eqToHom (by grind)⟩⟩
222	  presentation i := q.presentation i.2.choose
223	
224	/-- If `M` is quasicoherent, it is locally generated by sections. -/
225	@[simps]
226	def localGeneratorsData {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (q : M.QuasicoherentData) :
227	    M.LocalGeneratorsData where
228	  I := q.I
229	  X := q.X
230	  coversTop := q.coversTop
231	  generators i := (q.presentation i).generators

233	/-- A (local) presentation of a sheaf of module `M` is a finite presentation
234	if each given presentation of `M.over (X i)` is a finite presentation. -/
235	class IsFinitePresentation {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (q : M.QuasicoherentData) : Prop where
236	  isFinite_presentation (i : q.I) : (q.presentation i).IsFinite := by infer_instance

304	data on the trivial cover. -/
305	@[simps]
306	def Presentation.quasicoherentData {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (P : Presentation M) :

308	  I := C

312	    intro _ f
313	    simpa [Sieve.top_apply, iff_true] using ⟨x, Nonempty.intro f⟩
314	  presentation x := P.map (pushforward (𝟙 (R.over x))) (by rfl)

358	/-- Given an cover `X` and a quasicoherent data for `M` restricted onto each `Mᵢ`, we may glue them
359	into a quasicoherent data of `M` itself. -/
360	noncomputable def QuasicoherentData.bind {R : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}}
361	    (M : SheafOfModules.{u} R) {I : Type u}
362	    (X : I → C) (hX : J.CoversTop X) (D : Π i, QuasicoherentData (M.over (X i))) :
363	    M.QuasicoherentData where
364	  I := Σ i, (D i).I
365	  X ij := ((D ij.1).X ij.2).left
366	  coversTop Y := J.transitive (hX Y) _ fun Z f ⟨i, ⟨g⟩⟩ ↦
367	      J.superset_covering ((Sieve.functorPushforward_ofObjects_le _ _ _).trans
368	      (Sieve.ofObjects_mono fun i' ↦ by aesop)) ((D i).coversTop (.mk g))
369	  presentation i :=
370	    letI e := pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence (Over.iteratedSliceEquiv ((D i.1).X i.2))
371	      (S := (R.over _).over _) (R := R.over _) (𝟙 _) (𝟙 _)
372	      (by ext : 2; exact R.1.map_id _) (by ext : 2; exact R.1.map_id _)
373	    (((D i.1).presentation i.2).map e.inverse (.refl _)).ofIsIso
374	      (e.fullyFaithfulFunctor.preimageIso
375	      (by exact e.counitIso.app ((M.over (X i.1)).over ((D i.1).X i.2)))).hom
376	
377	lemma IsQuasicoherent.of_coversTop {R : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}}
378	    (M : SheafOfModules.{u} R) {I : Type u}
379	    (X : I → C) (hX : J.CoversTop X) [∀ i, IsQuasicoherent (M.over (X i))] :
380	    IsQuasicoherent M :=
381	  (QuasicoherentData.bind M X hX fun _ ↦
382	    IsQuasicoherent.nonempty_quasicoherentData.some).isQuasicoherent
383	
384	end bind

2722	sheaf of modules `M` on `Spec R` and `f : R`, the global-to-`D(f)` section restriction

2726	finite basic-open cover `exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData` refining the

2731	theorem isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : (Spec R).Modules)

2733	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
2734	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
2735	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom := by
2736	  obtain ⟨q⟩ := hqc.nonempty_quasicoherentData
2737	  obtain ⟨t, hspan, hcov⟩ := exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData M q

2739	  intro s hs_ex
2740	  obtain ⟨r, hr, hsr⟩ := hs_ex
2741	  obtain ⟨i, hri⟩ := hcov r hr
2742	  exact section_localization_hfr_basicOpen M q f s i (le_trans hsr hri)

2759	`f : R`, the section restriction `Γ(M, ⊤) → Γ(M, D(f))` exhibits the target as the localized module

3082	/-- **The annihilator ideal sheaf agrees with the module annihilator on affine opens**
3083	(`lem:modules_annihilator_ideal`). For a quasi-coherent `F` whose section module `Γ(F, V)` is
3084	finitely generated over `Γ(X, V)` on every affine open `V` (the affine-local finiteness supplied,
3085	for a finite-type `F`, by the G1 base case `lem:gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections`), the section over
3086	an affine open `U` of the annihilator ideal sheaf equals the module annihilator of the sections:
3087	`(annihilator F).ideal U = Ann_{Γ(X,U)}(Γ(F,U))`.
3088	
3089	The forward inclusion `≤` is `annihilator_ideal_le` (always available from `ofIdeals`). The reverse
3090	inclusion is genuinely *global*: `annihilator F = ofIdeals (V ↦ Ann_{Γ(X,V)}(Γ(F,V)))` is the
3091	**largest** ideal sheaf contained in the annihilator family, and its value at `U` reaches the full
3092	`Ann_{Γ(X,U)}(Γ(F,U))` precisely when that family is itself a coherent ideal sheaf — i.e. when it
3093	satisfies `IdealSheafData.map_ideal_basicOpen` at *every* affine open. That coherence is
3094	`annihilator_map_basicOpen`, which needs finite generation of `Γ(F, V)` at each `V`; hence the
3095	`hfin` hypothesis. With it the family assembles into an honest `IdealSheafData I` whose `ideal` is
3096	the annihilator family, and `IdealSheafData.ofIdeals_ideal` gives `ofIdeals I.ideal = I`, whence the
3097	claim at every `U` at once. This mirrors Mathlib's `Scheme.Hom.ker_apply`, which likewise builds the
3098	full ideal sheaf and reads off the affine value (using `QuasiCompact f` as its global hypothesis).
3099	Project-local because `annihilator` is. -/
3100	theorem annihilator_ideal (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
3101	    (hfin : ∀ V : X.affineOpens, Module.Finite Γ(X, V.1) Γ(F, V.1)) (U : X.affineOpens) :
3102	    (annihilator F).ideal U = Module.annihilator Γ(X, U.1) Γ(F, U.1) := by
3103	  let I : X.IdealSheafData :=
3104	    ⟨fun V => Module.annihilator Γ(X, V.1) Γ(F, V.1), ?_, _, rfl⟩
3105	  · exact congr($(IdealSheafData.ofIdeals_ideal I).ideal U)
3106	  · intro V f
3107	    haveI := hfin V
3108	    exact annihilator_map_basicOpen F V f
3109

1941	      ((LinearMap.map_smul _ (f ^ P) (f ^ (N - m r) • a r)).trans
1942	        ((congrArg (f ^ P • ·) (hb r)).trans
1943	          ((mul_smul (f ^ P) (f ^ N) _).symm.trans (congrArg (· • _) (pow_add f P N).symm))))))
1944	  exact (LinearMap.map_smul _ (f ^ (P + N)) y).trans hRHS.symm
1945	
1946	/-- **Section-localization descent from a local-tilde cover (gap1 keystone, D), cover form.**  Let
1947	`M` be a sheaf of modules on `Spec R` and `f : R`.  Suppose `{D(r)}_{r ∈ t}` is a finite basic-open
1948	cover of `Spec R` (`Ideal.span t = ⊤`) and for every open `U` contained in some cover member `D(r)`
1949	(in particular each `D(r)` and each overlap `D(r) ⊓ D(r')`) the basic-open restriction
1950	`Γ(M, U) → Γ(M, D(f) ⊓ U)` is a localization at `powers f` (`Hfr` — the gated P1 local-tilde data).
1951	Then the section restriction `Γ(M, ⊤) → Γ(M, D(f))` is `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `R`.
1952	
1953	This is the project-internal finite-equalizer/flatness descent of Stacks `lemma-invert-f-sections`
1954	(Hartshorne II.5.3), built without the global affine `QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R` equivalence (which is
1955	gap1 itself): `map_units` holds for arbitrary `M` (`map_units_restrict_basicOpen`), `surj'` is
1956	`descent_surj`, and `exists_of_eq` is `descent_smul_eq_zero`.  The named gap1 keystone
1957	`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent` for quasi-coherent `M` is this lemma instantiated at the cover
1958	`exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData` once `Hfr` is produced from
1959	`isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen` (the slice→Spec-`R_r` section transport, the remaining gated
1960	step).  Project-local. -/
1961	theorem isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent_of_cover {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : (Spec R).Modules)
1962	    (f : R) (t : Finset R) (hspan : Ideal.span (t : Set R) = ⊤)
1963	    (Hfr : ∀ U : (Spec R).Opens, (∃ r ∈ t, U ≤ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r) →
1964	      IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
1965	        ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
1966	          (homOfLE (inf_le_right : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ⊓ U ≤ U)).op).hom) :
1967	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
1968	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
1969	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom where
1970	  map_units := map_units_restrict_basicOpen M f
1971	  surj y := by
1972	    obtain ⟨x, n, hxn⟩ := descent_surj M f t hspan (fun U _ hcov => Hfr U hcov) y
1973	    exact ⟨⟨x, ⟨f ^ n, n, rfl⟩⟩, hxn⟩
1974	  exists_of_eq {x₁ x₂} h := by
1975	    have hθ : ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
1976	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom (x₁ - x₂) = 0 := by
1977	      rw [map_sub, h, sub_self]
1978	    obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := descent_smul_eq_zero M f t hspan
1979	      (fun r hr => Hfr (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r) ⟨r, hr, le_refl _⟩) (x₁ - x₂) hθ
1980	    exact ⟨⟨f ^ n, n, rfl⟩, sub_eq_zero.mp ((smul_sub (f ^ n) x₁ x₂).symm.trans hn)⟩
1981	
1982	/-- **Section-localization descent from a local-tilde cover, basic-open hypothesis form.**
1983	
1984	Same conclusion as `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent_of_cover`, but the per-cover-element
1985	localization data `Hfr` need only be supplied for *basic* opens `D(s) ≤ D(r)` (rather than every
1986	open `U ≤ D(r)`).  This is the **instantiable** form of the cover-descent: the per-element P1
1987	transport produces a localization only on the basic opens of the affine slice `Spec R_r` — a general
1988	open of `Spec R_r` need not be quasi-compact, so the global Stacks `lemma-invert-f-sections` is
1989	unavailable for it — while the sheaf-gluing engines `descent_surj`/`descent_smul_eq_zero` only ever
1990	consult `Hfr` at the basic opens `D(r)` and the overlaps `D(r) ⊓ D(r') = D(r·r')`.
1991	
1992	It rebuilds the three `IsLocalizedModule` fields directly: `map_units` is
1993	`map_units_restrict_basicOpen` (holds for arbitrary `M`), `surj` is `descent_surj` fed the basic-open
1994	`Hfr` (the open `U` it consults is always `D(s)`, so `Hfr s` supplies the datum after `U = D(s)` is
1995	substituted), and `exists_of_eq` is `descent_smul_eq_zero` fed `Hfr` at each `D(r)`.  Project-local:
1996	the named gap1 keystone `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent` for quasi-coherent `M` is this lemma
1997	instantiated at the cover `exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData`, with the basic-open
1998	`Hfr` produced from the P1 transport `isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen`. -/
1999	theorem isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent_of_basicOpen_cover {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
2000	    (M : (Spec R).Modules) (f : R) (t : Finset R) (hspan : Ideal.span (t : Set R) = ⊤)
2001	    (Hfr : ∀ s : R, (∃ r ∈ t, (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen s : (Spec R).Opens)
2002	        ≤ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r) →
2003	      IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
2004	        ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
2005	          (homOfLE (inf_le_right : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ⊓ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen s
2006	            ≤ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen s)).op).hom) :
2007	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
2008	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
2009	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom where
2010	  map_units := map_units_restrict_basicOpen M f
2011	  surj y := by
2012	    obtain ⟨x, n, hxn⟩ := descent_surj M f t hspan
2013	      (fun U hbo hcov => by obtain ⟨s, rfl⟩ := hbo; exact Hfr s hcov) y
2014	    exact ⟨⟨x, ⟨f ^ n, n, rfl⟩⟩, hxn⟩
2015	  exists_of_eq {x₁ x₂} h := by
2016	    have hθ : ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
2017	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom (x₁ - x₂) = 0 := by
2018	      rw [map_sub, h, sub_self]
2019	    obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := descent_smul_eq_zero M f t hspan
2020	      (fun r hr => Hfr r ⟨r, hr, le_refl _⟩) (x₁ - x₂) hθ
2021	    exact ⟨⟨f ^ n, n, rfl⟩, sub_eq_zero.mp ((smul_sub (f ^ n) x₁ x₂).symm.trans hn)⟩
2022	
2023	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `IsLocalizedModule` transport for gap1-D Hfr
2024	
2025	The section-transport iso `gammaPullbackImageIso` is only an additive-group (`Ab`) isomorphism,
2026	semilinear over the *source-scheme* section ring, whereas the `Hfr` hypothesis of
2027	`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent_of_cover` is an `IsLocalizedModule` statement `R`-linear over
2028	the base ring `R`. Two Mathlib-absent transport ingredients bridge the gap:
2029	
2030	* **(I)** `isLocalizedModule_of_ringEquiv_semilinear` — transport `IsLocalizedModule S g` across a
2031	  ring-iso-`σ`-semilinear `AddEquiv` pair. Mathlib only has the same-ring `of_linearEquiv` /
2032	  `of_linearEquiv_right`; the section iso crosses a ring iso, so this is the genuine gap.
2033	* **(II)** `isLocalizedModule_restrictScalars_powers_algebraMap` — a localization at
2034	  `powers (algebraMap R Rr f)` over a base-changed ring `Rr` (here `R` localized at `r`) is, after
2035	  restriction of scalars, a localization at `powers f` over `R`.
2036	
2037	Both are pure module algebra and Mathlib-absent at the pinned commit; project-bespoke. -/
2038	
2039	/-- **(I) Ring-iso-semilinear `IsLocalizedModule` transport.** Given a ring isomorphism
2040	`σ : R ≃+* R'`, two `σ`-semilinear additive isomorphisms `e₁ : M₁ ≃+ N₁`, `e₂ : M₂ ≃+ N₂` (i.e.
2041	`eᵢ (a • x) = σ a • eᵢ x`), and an `R'`-linear map `h : N₁ →ₗ[R'] N₂` intertwining a localization
2042	map `g` with the `eᵢ` (`h (e₁ x) = e₂ (g x)`), the map `h` is a localization at the image submonoid
2043	`S.map σ`. Mathlib only provides the same-ring `IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv`; this crosses a
2044	ring iso, the form needed to turn the `Ab`/semilinear section iso `gammaPullbackImageIso` into the
2045	`R`-linear `Hfr` data. Project-local. -/
2046	theorem isLocalizedModule_of_ringEquiv_semilinear
2047	    {R R' : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing R'] (σ : R ≃+* R')
2048	    {M₁ M₂ N₁ N₂ : Type*}
2049	    [AddCommGroup M₁] [Module R M₁] [AddCommGroup M₂] [Module R M₂]

        map_add' := fun t u => map_add _ t u

            (le_of_eq hV.fromSpec_preimage_self.symm)).map_smul r t

      (le_of_eq hV.fromSpec_preimage_self.symm) t := fun t => rfl

    have h : ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom b') = ⇑(pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap hV.fromSpec N
        (le_of_eq hV.fromSpec_preimage_self.symm)) := funext hb'app

  -- `toOpen` naturality plus the counit computation at `⊤`.

    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hV.fromSpec).obj N)))).symm, fun s => ?_⟩⟩
  have hinv : (((asIso (tilde.map b')) ≪≫ (asIso (Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ
      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hV.fromSpec).obj N)))).symm).inv =

  have hcounit := congrArg
    (fun (φ : (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj
        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hV.fromSpec).obj N)).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤) ⟶ _) =>

/-- **Step 3 pin (transport)**: section-level transport for pullback along
the affine-open's `fromSpec` map.

iter-189 Lane F unbundling (per `analogies/lane-f-isbasechange.md`
Decision 4): pinned as a separately-named typed sorry parallel to
`pullback_tildeIso` (Step 2) and `tildeIso_of_isQuasicoherent_isAffineOpen`
(Step 1).

This pin captures the Step 3 transport content of the Tilde-isoTop route:
the top section of a sheaf pulled back along
`IsAffineOpen.fromSpec : Spec Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Y` is canonically `Γ(Y, U)`-linearly
identified with the section over `U` itself. Substantive content combines
`AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.isoTop` (Mathlib HAS) with the `hU.isoSpec`
transport (Mathlib gap at `b80f227`).

iter-190 closure (Lane F Step 3 HARD BAR): the body chains
`Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback` (Mathlib's identification of the
`pullback` functor with the `restrict` functor along an open immersion;
applicable since `hU.fromSpec` carries `IsOpenImmersion` via
`IsAffineOpen.isOpenImmersion_fromSpec`) with the definitional
`Scheme.Modules.restrict_obj` (sections of `N.restrict f` over `V` equal
sections of `N` over `f ''ᵁ V`, by `rfl`) and the propositional
`Scheme.Hom.image_top_eq_opensRange` + `IsAffineOpen.opensRange_fromSpec`
to identify `hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤ = U`. -/
private theorem pullback_of_openImmersion_iso_restrict
    {Y : Scheme.{u}} (N : Y.Modules) {U : Y.Opens} (hU : IsAffineOpen U) :
    -- `Γ(Y, U)`-linear identification between the top section of the pullback
    -- (along `hU.fromSpec : Spec Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Y`) and `Γ(N, U)` itself. The
    -- module-action ring on the LHS is set up via the canonical algebra
    -- `Γ(Y, U) → Γ((Spec Γ(Y, U)), ⊤)`, which is the structure-sheaf
    -- equivalence on the affine scheme.
    letI : Algebra Γ(Y, U) Γ((Spec Γ(Y, U)), ⊤) :=
      (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom.toAlgebra
    letI : Module Γ(Y, U) Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N, ⊤) :=
      Module.compHom _ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom

===
    `(pullback (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde Γ(N, V)) ≅
      tilde (Γ(Y, U) ⊗ Γ(N, V))` on `Spec Γ(Y, U)`.
  Step 3: transport via `hU.isoSpec` back to `U`-sections of
    `(pullback g).obj N`.
  Step 4: evaluate at `⊤` via `tilde.isoTop` to extract the section-level
    linear equiv.
  Step 5: verify the intertwining via naturality of the adjunction unit
    (the Beck-Chevalley compatibility check; ~30-50 LOC).

The substantive Mathlib gap content (Stacks 01HQ "pullback of tilde =
tilde of base change", plus the affine-open / Spec transport) is
factored into the present helper's body as a typed sorry. Once
`pullback_tildeIso` lands axiom-clean (iter-189+ sub-build) and the
transport infrastructure is in place, this helper closes axiom-clean
in ~30-50 LOC. -/
private theorem pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap_sectionLinearEquiv
    {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X) (N : X.Modules) [N.IsQuasicoherent]
    {U : Y.Opens} {V : X.Opens}
    (_hU : IsAffineOpen U) (_hV : IsAffineOpen V)
    (e : U ≤ g ⁻¹ᵁ V) :
    letI : Algebra Γ(X, V) Γ(Y, U) := (g.appLE V U e).hom.toAlgebra
    letI : Module Γ(X, V) Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj N, U) :=
      Module.compHom _ (g.appLE V U e).hom
    Nonempty {f : TensorProduct Γ(X, V) Γ(Y, U) Γ(N, V) ≃ₗ[Γ(Y, U)]
                Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj N, U) //
      ∀ x : Γ(N, V),
        f (1 ⊗ₜ[Γ(X, V)] x) = pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap g N e x} := by
  letI : Algebra Γ(X, V) Γ(Y, U) := (g.appLE V U e).hom.toAlgebra
  letI : Module Γ(X, V) Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj N, U) :=
    Module.compHom _ (g.appLE V U e).hom
  -- iter-189 Lane F unbundle (per `analogies/lane-f-isbasechange.md`
  -- Decision 4): three Mathlib gaps are now pinned as separately-named
  -- typed sorries; the body of `_sectionLinearEquiv` is reduced to
  -- compositional bookkeeping over the chain.
  --
  -- Step 1 (Stacks 01I8 — `tildeIso_of_isQuasicoherent_isAffineOpen`):
  --   `N|_{Spec Γ(X, V)} ≅ tilde Γ(N, V)`  on  `Spec Γ(X, V)`.
  -- Pulling back along `Spec.map φ : Spec Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Spec Γ(X, V)`
  -- (where `φ = g.appLE V U e`) and applying Step 2 (`pullback_tildeIso`,
  -- Stacks 01HQ) gives `(Spec.map φ)^* tilde Γ(N, V) ≅
  --   tilde (Γ(Y, U) ⊗_{Γ(X, V)} Γ(N, V))`.
  -- Identifying the two compositions via the commutative square
  -- `hU.fromSpec ≫ g = Spec.map φ ≫ hV.fromSpec` and applying Step 3
  -- transport (`pullback_of_openImmersion_iso_restrict`) brings the
  -- section back to `U` itself. Evaluating tilde at `⊤` via
  -- `tilde.isoTop` extracts the section-level data; the underlying
  -- module of `tilde (Γ(Y, U) ⊗ Γ(N, V))` at `⊤` is exactly
  -- `Γ(Y, U) ⊗_{Γ(X, V)} Γ(N, V)`. The intertwining at `1 ⊗ x` (the
  -- Beck-Chevalley check) follows from naturality of the adjunction
  -- unit `pullback_app_isoTensor_unitAtV`.
  obtain ⟨⟨step1, _step1_apply⟩⟩ :=
    tildeIso_of_isQuasicoherent_isAffineOpen N _hV
  obtain ⟨⟨step2, _step2_apply⟩⟩ :=
    pullback_tildeIso (g.appLE V U e) (ModuleCat.of Γ(X, V) Γ(N, V))
  obtain ⟨step3⟩ :=
    pullback_of_openImmersion_iso_restrict
      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj N) _hU
  -- iter-193 Lane F: assemble the iso chain at the sheaf level.
  -- The commutative square `hU.fromSpec ≫ g = Spec.map φ ≫ hV.fromSpec`
  -- (where `φ = g.appLE V U e`) comes from Mathlib's
  -- `IsAffineOpen.SpecMap_appLE_fromSpec`.
  have h_eq : _hU.fromSpec ≫ g = Spec.map (g.appLE V U e) ≫ _hV.fromSpec :=
    (IsAffineOpen.SpecMap_appLE_fromSpec g _hV _hU e).symm
  -- Sheaf-level iso chain (5-step compositional transport):
  --   (pullback hU.fromSpec).obj ((pullback g).obj N)
  -- = (pullback g ⋙ pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N                         [defeq]
  -- ≅ (pullback (hU.fromSpec ≫ g)).obj N             [pullbackComp]
  -- ≅ (pullback (Spec.map φ ≫ hV.fromSpec)).obj N    [pullbackCongr h_eq]
  -- ≅ (pullback (Spec.map φ)).obj ((pullback hV.fromSpec).obj N)
  --                                                   [(pullbackComp).symm]
  -- ≅ (pullback (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde Γ(N, V))    [step1 (Stacks 01I8)]
  -- ≅ tilde (TensorProduct Γ(X,V) Γ(Y,U) Γ(N,V))     [step2 (Stacks 01HQ)]
  let composedIso :=
    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp _hU.fromSpec g).app N ≪≫
      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr h_eq).app N ≪≫
      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (Spec.map (g.appLE V U e)) _hV.fromSpec).app N).symm ≪≫
      (Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map (g.appLE V U e))).mapIso step1 ≪≫
      step2)

3700	  -- (a) chain `topAdd` with `tilde.isoTop.symm` to land in TensorProduct;
3701	  -- (b) upgrade AddEquiv → Γ(Y, U)-LinearEquiv via Hom.app_smul + ΓSpecIso;
3702	  -- (c) compose with `step3` to reach Γ((pullback g).obj N, U);
3703	  -- (d) verify the Beck-Chevalley intertwining `1 ⊗ x ↦ baseMap g N e x`
3704	  --     using naturality of the adjunction unit `pullback_app_isoTensor_unitAtV`.
3705	  let topAdd :=
3706	    { toFun := fun x => (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app composedIso.hom ⊤).hom x
3707	      invFun := fun y => (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app composedIso.inv ⊤).hom y
3708	      left_inv := fun x => by
3709	        simp only [← AddCommGrpCat.comp_apply,
3710	          ← Scheme.Modules.Hom.comp_app, composedIso.hom_inv_id,
3711	          Scheme.Modules.Hom.id_app, AddCommGrpCat.hom_id, AddMonoidHom.id_apply]
3712	      right_inv := fun y => by
3713	        simp only [← AddCommGrpCat.comp_apply,
3714	          ← Scheme.Modules.Hom.comp_app, composedIso.inv_hom_id,
3715	          Scheme.Modules.Hom.id_app, AddCommGrpCat.hom_id, AddMonoidHom.id_apply]
3716	      map_add' := fun x y =>
3717	        (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app composedIso.hom ⊤).hom.map_add x y
3718	      : Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback _hU.fromSpec).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj N), ⊤) ≃+ _ }
3719	  -- iter-194 Lane F LinearEquiv extraction (PUSH-BEYOND, axiom-clean):

3721	  --     `Module.compHom _ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom` recipe.
3722	  -- (b) Compose with `(tilde.isoTop _).symm.toLinearEquiv` to land in the
3723	  --     TensorProduct module (the underlying type is the same as
3724	  --     `(modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde _)).presheaf.obj (.op ⊤)`, and the
3725	  --     `Γ(Y, U)`-module structures agree by `Module.compHom`/`restrictScalars`
3726	  --     defeq).
3727	  -- (c) Compose with `step3` to land in `Γ((pullback g).obj N, U)`.
3728	  -- (d) Beck-Chevalley intertwining: typed sorry (see ARCHITECTURAL NOTE below).
3729	  -- Introduce a local alias for the target ModuleCat to avoid Γ-notation
3730	  -- ambiguity inside type ascriptions.
3731	  let TR : ModuleCat (Γ(Y, U)) :=

3746	        ≃ₗ[Γ(Y, U)] Γ(tilde TR, ⊤) := by
3747	    refine topAdd.toLinearEquiv ?_
3748	    intro r x
3749	    -- Module.compHom on both sides: r • _ = (ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom r • _.
3750	    change (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app composedIso.hom ⊤).hom
3751	      ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom r • x) =

3764	  intro x

3766	  --

3804	  -- signatures pinning the four named substrate helpers (N1)-(N4) for
3805	  -- iter-196 prover.

2581	an inclusion of opens `i : V ⟶ U`, the presheaf restriction `Γ(M, U) → Γ(M, V)` is `Γ(X, U)`-linear

2587	    letI : Module Γ(X, U) Γ(M, V) := Module.compHom _ (X.presheaf.map i.op).hom
2588	    Γ(M, U) →ₗ[Γ(X, U)] Γ(M, V) :=
2589	  letI : Module Γ(X, U) Γ(M, V) := Module.compHom _ (X.presheaf.map i.op).hom
2590	  { toFun := fun x => M.presheaf.map i.op x
2591	    map_add' := map_add _
2592	    map_smul' := fun r x => Scheme.Modules.map_smul M i r x }
2593	
2594	/-- **The `Γ(X,U)`-linear restriction of sections to a basic open `D(f)`** (`f : Γ(X, U)`). The
2595	section restriction `Γ(M, U) → Γ(M, X.basicOpen f)` is `Γ(X, U)`-linear, where `Γ(M, X.basicOpen f)`
2596	carries any `Γ(X, U)`-module structure compatible (via `IsScalarTower`) with its native
2597	`Γ(X, X.basicOpen f)`-module structure and the canonical `Γ(X, U)`-algebra map
2598	`Γ(X, U) → Γ(X, X.basicOpen f)` (the restriction `X.presheaf.map`). Linearity combines
2599	`Scheme.Modules.map_smul` with the scalar tower. This is the consumer-facing shape of the gap2
2600	keystone (instances supplied by the caller, matching `Module.annihilator_isLocalizedModule_eq_map`).
2601	Project-local. -/
2602	noncomputable def restrictBasicOpenₗ {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) {U : X.Opens} (f : Γ(X, U))
2603	    [Module Γ(X, U) Γ(M, X.basicOpen f)]
2604	    [IsScalarTower Γ(X, U) Γ(X, X.basicOpen f) Γ(M, X.basicOpen f)] :
2605	    Γ(M, U) →ₗ[Γ(X, U)] Γ(M, X.basicOpen f) where
2606	  toFun := fun x => M.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f)).op x
2607	  map_add' := map_add _
2608	  map_smul' := fun r x => by
2609	    change M.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f)).op (r • x) = r • _
2610	    rw [Scheme.Modules.map_smul M (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f)) r x,
2611	      ← algebraMap_smul Γ(X, X.basicOpen f) r (M.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f)).op x)]
2612	    rfl
2613	
2614	/-- **`fromSpec`-section coherence** (gap2 transport crux). For an affine open `U` of a scheme `X`,
2615	the `eqToHom`-transport `Γ(X, hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤) → Γ(X, U)` (along the equality
2616	`hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤ = U`) equals the composite ring iso
2617	`(hU.fromSpec.appIso ⊤).hom ≫ (ΓSpecIso Γ(X, U)).hom`. Equivalently, the section ring iso
2618	`σ = (ΓSpecIso)⁻¹ ≫ gammaImageRingEquiv (fromSpec) ⊤` underlying the gap2 section comparison is, up to
2619	this `eqToHom` transport, the identity. This is the coherence needed to read the gap2-core
2620	localization (over `Γ(X, hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤)`, at `powers (σ f)`) back as a localization over

2622	`Spec Γ(X, U)`-presheaf maps (all between `⊤`, hence forced by `Subsingleton`). Project-local. -/
2623	theorem fromSpec_image_top_section_coherence {X : Scheme.{u}} {U : X.Opens} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
2624	    (eT : hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ (⊤ : (Spec Γ(X, U)).Opens) = U) :

2626	      = (hU.fromSpec.appIso (⊤ : (Spec Γ(X, U)).Opens)).hom ≫ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso Γ(X, U)).hom := by
2627	  rw [← cancel_epi (X.presheaf.map (eqToHom eT).op),
2628	    ← X.presheaf.map_comp, ← op_comp, eqToHom_trans, eqToHom_refl, op_id, X.presheaf.map_id]
2629	  rw [Scheme.Hom.appIso_hom', Scheme.Hom.appLE]
2630	  have hnat := hU.fromSpec.naturality (eqToHom eT).op
2631	  simp only [Category.assoc]
2632	  rw [reassoc_of% hnat, hU.fromSpec_app_self]
2633	  simp only [eqToHom_unop, eqToHom_map, eqToHom_op]
2634	  simp only [Category.assoc]
2635	  rw [eqToHom_trans_assoc, ← eqToHom_map (Spec Γ(X, U)).presheaf,
2636	    ← (Spec Γ(X, U)).presheaf.map_comp_assoc]
2637	  · rw [Subsingleton.elim (eqToHom _ ≫ (homOfLE _).op)
2638	      (𝟙 (Opposite.op (⊤ : (Spec Γ(X, U)).Opens))),

private lemma descent_smul_eq_zero {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : (Spec R).Modules) (f : R)
    (t : Finset R) (hspan : Ideal.span (t : Set R) = ⊤)
    (Hfr : ∀ r ∈ t, IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
        (homOfLE (inf_le_right :
          PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ⊓ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r
            ≤ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r)).op).hom)
    (x : ToType ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.obj (.op ⊤)))
    (hx : ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom x = 0) :
    ∃ n : ℕ, f ^ n • x = 0 := by
  classical
  have key : ∀ r : {x // x ∈ t}, ∃ k : ℕ, f ^ k •
      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (r:R) ≤ ⊤)).op).hom x = 0 := by
    rintro ⟨r, hr⟩
    have e1 := res_comp (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M)
        (A := ⊤) (B := PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r)
        (C := PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ⊓ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r) le_top inf_le_right le_top x
    have e2 := res_comp (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M)
        (A := ⊤) (B := PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)
        (C := PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ⊓ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r) le_top inf_le_left le_top x
    have hzero := e1.trans (e2.symm.trans
      ((congrArg (((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
          (homOfLE (inf_le_left :
            PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ⊓ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r
              ≤ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)).op).hom) hx).trans
        (map_zero _)))
    obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := (Hfr r hr).exists_of_eq (hzero.trans (map_zero _).symm)
    obtain ⟨k, hk⟩ := c.2
    have hk' : f ^ k = (c : R) := hk
    refine ⟨k, ?_⟩
    have h2 : c • (((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
          (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (r:R) ≤ ⊤)).op).hom x) = 0 :=
      hc.trans (smul_zero c)
    rw [hk']; exact h2
  choose k hk using key
  refine ⟨Finset.univ.sup k, ?_⟩
  refine TopCat.Sheaf.eq_of_locally_eq' (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M)
    (fun r : {x // x ∈ t} => (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (r:R) : (Spec R).Opens)) ⊤
    (fun r => homOfLE le_top) (le_of_eq (iSup_basicOpen_subtype_eq_top hspan).symm)
    (f ^ Finset.univ.sup k • x) 0 ?_
  intro r
  have hle : k r ≤ Finset.univ.sup k := Finset.le_sup (Finset.mem_univ r)
  set g := ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (r:R) ≤ ⊤)).op).hom with hg
  have hms : g (f ^ Finset.univ.sup k • x) = f ^ Finset.univ.sup k • g x := LinearMap.map_smul g _ x
  have hsplit : f ^ Finset.univ.sup k • g x
      = f ^ (Finset.univ.sup k - k r) • (f ^ (k r) • g x) := by
    rw [← mul_smul, ← pow_add, Nat.sub_add_cancel hle]
  have hzero : g (f ^ Finset.univ.sup k • x) = 0 :=
    hms.trans (hsplit.trans ((congrArg (fun y => f ^ (Finset.univ.sup k - k r) • y) (hk r)).trans
      (smul_zero _)))
  change g (f ^ Finset.univ.sup k • x) = g 0
  rw [hzero, map_zero]

/-- **Overlap agreement for the surjectivity field.**  If a section `br` on `D(r)` satisfies the
normalized identity `ρ[D(r), D(f) ⊓ D(r)] br = f^N • (y|_{D(f) ⊓ D(r)})`, then for any open
`U ≤ D(r)` its restriction to `U`, pushed down to `D(f) ⊓ U`, equals `f^N • (y|_{D(f) ⊓ U})`.
Specializing `U` to an overlap `D(r) ⊓ D(r')` shows the normalized sections of two cover members
agree there after restriction to `D(f) ⊓ (D(r) ⊓ D(r'))`, which (via the per-overlap localization)

`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_isQuasicoherent`) — then the tilde-Gamma counit `M.fromTildeΓ` is
an isomorphism (equivalently `M` lies in the essential image of `tilde`).

On each basic open `D(f)` the component of `modulesSpecToSheaf.map M.fromTildeΓ` is a map between
two localizations of `N = Γ(M, ⊤)` at `powers f`: the source `Γ(tilde N, D(f))` localizes via the
instance `tilde.toOpen N (D f)` and the target `Γ(M, D(f))` localizes by hypothesis, and the two
localization maps are intertwined by `Scheme.Modules.toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app`. Hence the component is
the canonical localization isomorphism (`IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv`);
`isIso_sheaf_of_isIso_app_basicOpen` upgrades this to an isomorphism of sheaves, and
`modulesSpecToSheaf` being fully faithful reflects it to `IsIso M.fromTildeΓ`.

This turns the remaining keystone obligation into exactly G1-core: combined with the converse engine
`isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ`, the per-basic-open localization hypothesis is
*equivalent* to `IsIso M.fromTildeΓ`. Project-local: Mathlib has no `IsQuasicoherent → IsIso
fromTildeΓ` bridge. -/
theorem isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
    (M : (Spec R).Modules)
    (H : ∀ f : R, IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom) :
    IsIso M.fromTildeΓ := by
  haveI hmain : IsIso (modulesSpecToSheaf.map M.fromTildeΓ) := by
    apply isIso_sheaf_of_isIso_app_basicOpen
    intro f
    set N := (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.obj (.op ⊤) with hN
    -- target localizes by hypothesis; source localizes by the `tilde.toOpen` instance
    haveI htgt : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
        ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
          (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom := H f
    set comp := (modulesSpecToSheaf.map M.fromTildeΓ).1.app (.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f))
      with hcomp
    rw [ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective]
    have hcompose := Scheme.Modules.toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)
    -- the component intertwines the two localization maps of `N` at `powers f`
    have h1 : comp.hom ∘ₗ (tilde.toOpen N (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)).hom
        = ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
          (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom := by
      have e := congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom hcompose
      rwa [ModuleCat.hom_comp] at e
    change Function.Bijective (⇑comp.hom)
    exact bijective_comp_of_localizations (Submonoid.powers f)
      (inferInstance : IsLocalizedModule _ (tilde.toOpen N (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)).hom)
      (H f) h1
  exact SpecModulesToSheafFullyFaithful.isIso_of_isIso_map M.fromTildeΓ

/-- **Characterization of `IsIso M.fromTildeΓ` by section localization.** For a sheaf of modules
`M` on `Spec R`, the tilde-Gamma counit `M.fromTildeΓ` is an isomorphism iff for every `f : R` the
section restriction `Γ(M, ⊤) → Γ(M, D(f))` exhibits the target as `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)`.

The forward direction is the affine engine `isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ`; the
reverse is `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict`. Combined with G1-core
(`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_isQuasicoherent`, `lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization`, not yet
formalized) — which supplies the right-hand side for any quasi-coherent `M` — this yields gap1
(`lem:qcoh_affine_isIso_fromTildeΓ`). Project-local. -/
theorem isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
    (M : (Spec R).Modules) :
    IsIso M.fromTildeΓ ↔ ∀ f : R, IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom :=
  ⟨fun h f => by haveI := h; exact isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ M f,
    isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict M⟩

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — G1-core (Route F) building blocks

The keystone G1-core `lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization` asks: for a *quasi-coherent*
`M : (Spec R).Modules` and `f : R`, the section restriction `Γ(M,⊤) → Γ(M,D(f))` is
`IsLocalizedModule (powers f)`. Via `isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict` this is
*equivalent* to `IsIso M.fromTildeΓ` — i.e. to the statement that a quasi-coherent sheaf on an
affine scheme lies in the essential image of `tilde` (the `QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R` equivalence). That
equivalence is a genuine Mathlib gap at the pinned commit (Tilde.lean only proves the *globally
presented* case, `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`).

The composition lemma below discharges the **globally-presented** sub-case end to end (it is the
Route-F endpoint once a global presentation/tilde identification is in hand). The residual gap is
exactly the production of a global presentation/tilde from local (quasi-coherent) data on `Spec R`;
see the handoff in `task_results/.../QuotScheme.md`. -/

/-- **Basic-open restriction localizes, for a globally presented module.** If `M : (Spec R).Modules`
admits a global `SheafOfModules.Presentation`, then for every `f : R` the section restriction
`Γ(M,⊤) → Γ(M,D(f))` exhibits the target as `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `R`.

This is the composition of Mathlib's `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation` (a global presentation
forces `M.fromTildeΓ` to be an isomorphism, i.e. `M` is a `tilde`) with the affine engine
`isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ`. It is the Route-F endpoint for the
globally-presented case; the general quasi-coherent case additionally requires producing a global
presentation/tilde identification from the (Mathlib-absent) affine `QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R` bridge.
Project-local. -/
theorem isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_presentation {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
    (M : (Spec R).Modules) (_P : M.Presentation) (f : R) :
    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom := by
  haveI : IsIso M.fromTildeΓ := isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation M _P
  exact isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ M f

/-- **`map_units` field of G1-core (Route F), for any sheaf of modules.** For `M : (Spec R).Modules`
and `f : R`, every element of `Submonoid.powers f` acts invertibly on the sections `Γ(M, D(f))` over
`R`. This is exactly the first field `IsLocalizedModule.map_units` of the target
`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_isQuasicoherent`, in the shape the 3-field constructor consumes.

It holds for an *arbitrary* `M` (no quasi-coherence needed): on `D(f)` the element `f` already maps
to a unit of the structure ring `Γ(O_{Spec R}, D(f))` (the away-localization
`IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit`), and the `R`-action on `Γ(M, D(f))` factors through it via
the scalar tower `R → Γ(O, D(f)) → Γ(M, D(f))`. Packaged from Mathlib's
`AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.isUnit_algebraMap_end_basicOpen`. Project-local only as the *named* field
of the Route-F decomposition; the substance of G1-core is `surj`/`exists_of_eq` (see handoff). -/
theorem map_units_restrict_basicOpen {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : (Spec R).Modules) (f : R) :

2130	    refine ⟨⟨f ^ n, n, rfl⟩, ?_⟩
2131	    have e1 : (f ^ n : R) • x₁ = (↑c : Rr) • x₁ := by rw [← hn', ← map_pow, algebraMap_smul]
2132	    have e2 : (f ^ n : R) • x₂ = (↑c : Rr) • x₂ := by rw [← hn', ← map_pow, algebraMap_smul]
2133	    change (f ^ n : R) • x₁ = (f ^ n : R) • x₂
2134	    rw [e1, e2]; exact hc

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
/-- Given a presentation of a module `M`, we may construct an associated presentation of `M^~`. -/
noncomputable
def presentationTilde (s : Set M) (hs : Submodule.span R s = ⊤)
    (t : Set (s →₀ R))
    (ht : Submodule.span R t = LinearMap.ker (Finsupp.linearCombination R ((↑) : s → M))) :
    (tilde M).Presentation := by
  haveI H₁ : Function.Exact
      (ModuleCat.ofHom (Finsupp.linearCombination (α := t) R (↑)))
      (ModuleCat.ofHom (Finsupp.linearCombination (α := s) (M := M) R (↑))) :=
    (LinearMap.exact_iff.mpr (by simp [Finsupp.range_linearCombination, ht]))
  refine SheafOfModules.presentationOfIsCokernelFree.{u}
      ((tildeFinsupp t).inv ≫ tilde.map (ModuleCat.ofHom (Finsupp.linearCombination R (↑))) ≫
        (tildeFinsupp s).hom) ((tildeFinsupp s).inv ≫
          tilde.map (ModuleCat.ofHom (Finsupp.linearCombination R (↑)))) (by
    simp only [Category.assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc, Preadditive.IsIso.comp_left_eq_zero]
    rw [← tilde.map_comp, ← ModuleCat.ofHom_comp]
    convert tilde.map_zero
    exact congr(ModuleCat.ofHom $(H₁.linearMap_comp_eq_zero))) ?_
  letI h₁ := ModuleCat.isColimitCokernelCofork _ _ H₁
    (by simp [← LinearMap.range_eq_top, Finsupp.range_linearCombination, hs])
  refine IsCokernel.ofIso _ (CokernelCofork.mapIsColimit _ h₁ (tilde.functor R)) _ (tildeFinsupp t)
    (tildeFinsupp s) (.refl _) (by simp) (by simp)

instance : (tilde M).IsQuasicoherent :=
  (presentationTilde.{u} _ .univ (by simp) _ (Submodule.span_eq _)).isQuasicoherent

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
lemma isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation (M : (Spec R).Modules) (P : M.Presentation) :
    IsIso M.fromTildeΓ := by
  rw [isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff]

lemma Scheme.Hom.isQuasiSeparated_preimage [QuasiSeparated f] {U : Opens Y}
    (hU : IsQuasiSeparated (U : Set Y)) : IsQuasiSeparated (f ⁻¹ᵁ U : Set X) := by
  have : QuasiSeparatedSpace U := (isQuasiSeparated_iff_quasiSeparatedSpace _ U.2).mp hU
  exact (isQuasiSeparated_iff_quasiSeparatedSpace _ (f ⁻¹ᵁ U).2).mpr
    (quasiSeparatedSpace_of_quasiSeparated (f ∣_ U))

instance quasiSeparatedSpace_of_isAffine (X : Scheme) [IsAffine X] : QuasiSeparatedSpace X :=

variable (X : Scheme) {V U : X.Opens} (f g : Γ(X, U))

/-- The subset of the underlying space where the given section does not vanish. -/
def basicOpen : X.Opens :=
  X.toLocallyRingedSpace.toRingedSpace.basicOpen f

theorem mem_basicOpen (x : X) (hx : x ∈ U) :
    x ∈ X.basicOpen f ↔ IsUnit (X.presheaf.germ U x hx f) :=
  RingedSpace.mem_basicOpen _ _ _ _

/-- A variant of `mem_basicOpen` for bundled `x : U`. -/
@[simp]
theorem mem_basicOpen' (x : U) : ↑x ∈ X.basicOpen f ↔ IsUnit (X.presheaf.germ U x x.2 f) :=
  RingedSpace.mem_basicOpen _ _ _ _

/-- A variant of `mem_basicOpen` without the `x ∈ U` assumption. -/
theorem mem_basicOpen'' {U : X.Opens} (f : Γ(X, U)) (x : X) :
    x ∈ X.basicOpen f ↔ ∃ (m : x ∈ U), IsUnit (X.presheaf.germ U x m f) :=
  Iff.rfl

theorem mem_basicOpen_top (f : Γ(X, ⊤)) (x : X) :
    x ∈ X.basicOpen f ↔ IsUnit (X.presheaf.germ ⊤ x trivial f) :=
  RingedSpace.mem_top_basicOpen _ f x

@[simp]
theorem basicOpen_res (i : op U ⟶ op V) : X.basicOpen (X.presheaf.map i f) = V ⊓ X.basicOpen f :=

data.content:
`U` of `X` (`⨆ i, U i = ⊤`), the global sections `Γ(X, M) = Γ(M, ⊤)`, regarded as a module
over the ground ring `A = Γ(X, ⊤)`, are `A`-linearly isomorphic to the `eqLocus` of the two
restriction legs `leftRes`, `rightRes : ∏ᵢ Γ(M, Uᵢ) → ∏ᵢⱼ Γ(M, Uᵢ ⊓ Uⱼ)`.

Project-local: this is the `LinearMap.eqLocus` presentation of global sections over the
ground ring `A` that `LinearMap.tensorEqLocusEquiv` (flat base change preserves finite
equalizers) consumes for the `H⁰` flat-base-change argument. Injectivity is the separatedness
of the sheaf of modules (`TopCat.Sheaf.eq_of_locally_eq'`) and surjectivity is the gluing
axiom (`TopCat.Sheaf.existsUnique_gluing'`), both on the underlying `Ab`-sheaf `M.presheaf`. -/
noncomputable def gammaTopEquivEqLocus {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) {ι : Type u}
    (U : ι → X.Opens) (hU : iSup U = ⊤) :
    gammaModA M (⊤ : X.Opens) ≃ₗ[groundRing X] LinearMap.eqLocus (leftRes M U) (rightRes M U) :=
  LinearEquiv.ofBijective (toCoverEqLocus M U)
    ⟨by
      intro s t hst
      have h := Subtype.ext_iff.mp hst
      refine TopCat.Sheaf.eq_of_locally_eq' (⟨M.presheaf, M.isSheaf⟩ : TopCat.Sheaf Ab X)
        U ⊤ (fun _ => homOfLE le_top) hU.ge _ _ ?_
      intro i
      have hi := congrFun h i
      simp only [toCoverEqLocus, LinearMap.codRestrict_apply, toCover, LinearMap.pi_apply,
        gammaResA_apply] at hi ⊢
      exact hi,
     by
      rintro ⟨sf, hsf⟩
      have hcompat : TopCat.Presheaf.IsCompatible M.presheaf U sf := by
        intro i j
        have h := congrFun hsf (i, j)
        simp only [leftRes, rightRes, LinearMap.pi_apply, LinearMap.comp_apply,
          LinearMap.proj_apply, gammaResA_apply] at h ⊢
        exact h
      obtain ⟨s, hs, -⟩ := TopCat.Sheaf.existsUnique_gluing'
        (⟨M.presheaf, M.isSheaf⟩ : TopCat.Sheaf Ab X) U ⊤ (fun _ => homOfLE le_top) hU.ge sf hcompat
      refine ⟨s, ?_⟩
      apply Subtype.ext
      funext i
      have h := hs i
      simp only [toCoverEqLocus, LinearMap.codRestrict_apply, toCover, LinearMap.pi_apply,
        gammaResA_apply] at h ⊢
      exact h⟩

/-- **Flat base change commutes with the `H⁰` equalizer.** For `M : X.Modules`, a cover `U`
of `X` (`⨆ i, U i = ⊤`), and a flat `A`-algebra `B` (`A = Γ(X, ⊤)`), base changing the global
sections `Γ(X, M)` along `A → B` is the `eqLocus` of the base-changed restriction legs:
\[ B ⊗_A Γ(X, M) ≅ \operatorname{eqLocus}(B ⊗ \mathrm{leftRes},\ B ⊗ \mathrm{rightRes}). \]

Project-local: the composite of the equalizer presentation
`gammaTopEquivEqLocus` with `LinearMap.tensorEqLocusEquiv` (flatness commutes with the finite
equalizer). This is the module-level core that the `H⁰` flat-base-change reduction consumes:
the right-hand side is, by the same presentation applied to the base-changed sheaf, the global
sections of the pulled-back module over `B`. -/
noncomputable def baseChangeGammaEquiv {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) {ι : Type u}
    (U : ι → X.Opens) (hU : iSup U = ⊤) (B : Type u) [CommRing B] [Algebra (groundRing X) B]
    [Module.Flat (groundRing X) B] :
    TensorProduct (groundRing X) B (gammaModA M (⊤ : X.Opens)) ≃ₗ[B]
      LinearMap.eqLocus (TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.lTensor B B (leftRes M U))

data.content:
/-
